LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS 3515-17-19 SACRAMENTO STREET AND ALICE FARRELLY'S MOTION TO CONTEST GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS "IN THE BALLPARK" UNDER TECH-BILT. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT SETTLING PARTY (BRUCE PARSLEY) WAS BASICALLY INSOLVENT AT THE TIME OF SETTLEMENT. FURTHER, AT THE TIME SETTLEMENT WAS REACHED IN 2009, THE REMAINING CO-DEFENDANTS HAD OBTAINED SUMMARY JUDGMENT; THEY WERE NOT PRESENT AND DID NOT PARTICIPATE IN THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF COLLUSION. ALTHOUGH THE DOLLAR AMOUNT IS SMALL, IT REPRESENTED AT THE TIME OF SETTLEMENT 100% OF THE RECOVERY PLAINTIFF WOULD RECEIVE, GIVEN THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT RULINGS AS TO THE OTHER CO-DEFENDANTS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE SETTLEMENT WAS IN GOOD FAITH. DEFENDANT PARSLEY'S COUNSEL PRESENTED AN ENDORSED FILE-STAMPED COPY OF NOTICE OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IN OPEN COURT WHICH WAS FILED TODAY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ANDREA DEBERRY'S MOTION TO CONTEST GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS "IN THE BALLPARK" UNDER TECH-BILT. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT SETTLING PARTY (BRUCE PARSLEY) WAS BASICALLY INSOLVENT AT THE TIME OF SETTLEMENT. FURTHER, AT THE TIME SETTLEMENT WAS REACHED IN 2009, THE REMAINING CO-DEFENDANTS HAD OBTAINED SUMMARY JUDGMENT; THEY WERE NOT PRESENT AND DID NOT PARTICIPATE IN THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF COLLUSION. ALTHOUGH THE DOLLAR AMOUNT IS SMALL, IT REPRESENTED AT THE TIME OF SETTLEMENT 100% OF THE RECOVERY PLAINTIFF WOULD RECEIVE, GIVEN THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT RULINGS AS TO THE OTHER CO-DEFENDANTS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE SETTLEMENT WAS IN GOOD FAITH. DEFENDANT PARSLEY'S COUNSEL PRESENTED AN ENDORSED FILE-STAMPED COPY OF NOTICE OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IN OPEN COURT WHICH WAS FILED TODAY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Andrea Deberry'S Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement Determination is denied. The Court finds that the settlement is "in the ballpark" under Tech-Bilt. The undisputed evidence establishes that settling party (Bruce Parsley) was basically insolvent at the time of settlement. Further, at the time settlement was reached in 2009, the remaining co-defendants had obtained summary judgment; they were not present and did not participate in the settlement conference. There is no evidence of collusion. Although the dollar amount is small, it represented at the time of settlement 100% of the recovery plaintiff would receive, given the summary judgment rulings as to the other co-defendants. The Court concludes that the settlement was in good faith. Defendant Parsley's counsel presented an endorsed file-stamped copy of Notice of Good Faith Settlement in open court which was filed today. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALICE FARRELLY AND 3515-17-19 SACRAMENTO STREET'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Alison M. Crane of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel & Pedersen (981.5411) for defendants and moving parties
Michael Keck of the Law Offices of C.L. Keck (861.2267) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: defendant's objection to exhibit 7 to the Keck Declaration is sustained based on the grounds of inadmissible hearsay and a lack of proper authentication in that there is a lack of personal knowledge to authenticate. Summary Judgment is granted based on the following reasons: Plaintiff's exclusive remedy against defendants was the worker's compensation remedy that the plaintiff pursued. If plaintiff was allowed to pursue this action in Court, his prior settlement would limit him to his claim that the defendants were negligent by reason of their own action or inaction and yet the undisputed evidence shows that no act or omission on the part of either defendant was a substantial factor in causing harm to the plaintiff. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 437c(g). (302/JEM/VC) Judge: John E. Munter; Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 956
